title: Introduction
seoTitle: Node SDK Introduction â€” Talonic Docs
description: Official Talonic SDK for Node.js and TypeScript. Extract structured, schema-validated data from any document with a single function call.

content.text: The `@talonic/node` SDK is the official Node.js and TypeScript client for the Talonic API. Extract structured, schema-validated data from any document with a single function call.
Zero runtime dependencies. Requires Node.js 18 or newer.

Looking for the AI agent path? [`@talonic/mcp`](https://github.com/talonicdev/talonic-mcp) wraps this SDK as a Model Context Protocol server. Install it into Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, Continue, or Cowork and any MCP-aware agent can extract documents directly.

faq.question: What is the Talonic Node SDK?
faq.answer: The official Node.js and TypeScript client for extracting structured, schema-validated data from documents via the Talonic API.

faq.question: What Node.js version is required?
faq.answer: Node.js 18 or newer. The SDK has zero runtime dependencies.

title: Installation
seoTitle: Install Talonic Node SDK â€” Talonic Docs
description: Install the @talonic/node package via npm. Zero runtime dependencies, requires Node.js 18+.

content.text: Requires Node.js 18 or newer. Zero runtime dependencies.

faq.question: How do I install the Talonic Node SDK?
faq.answer: Run npm install @talonic/node. Requires Node.js 18 or newer. Zero runtime dependencies.

title: Authentication
description: Get a Talonic API key and configure it for the Node SDK. Each workspace is isolated with private documents and schemas.

content.text: Every user runs against their own Talonic workspace, so each user needs their own key. Workspaces are isolated; your documents and schemas are private to you.

items: Sign up at [https://app.talonic.com](https://app.talonic.com). Free tier: 50 extractions per day, no credit card.
Settings → API Keys → Create New Key.
Set it as the `TALONIC_API_KEY` environment variable, or pass it directly to the client constructor.

content.text: Keep your API key secret. Do not expose it in client-side code or version control.

faq.question: Where do I get a Talonic API key?
faq.answer: Sign up at app.talonic.com, go to Settings â†’ API Keys â†’ Create New Key. Free tier includes 50 extractions per day.

title: Quick Start
seoTitle: Node SDK Quick Start â€” Talonic Docs
description: Extract structured data from a document in five lines of TypeScript using the Talonic Node SDK.

content.title: Extract an invoice

faq.question: How do I extract data from a document with the SDK?
faq.answer: Call talonic.extract() with a file_path and a schema defining the fields you want. Returns structured JSON with confidence scores.

title: Configuration
description: Configure the Talonic client with API key, base URL, timeout, max retries, and custom fetch function.

content.title: Client configuration
content.code:
const talonic = new Talonic({
  apiKey: process.env.TALONIC_API_KEY!,
  baseUrl: "https://api.talonic.com",   // default
  timeout: 60_000,                       // ms; default 60s
  maxRetries: 3,                         // 429, 500, 502, 503, 504, network, timeout
  fetch: customFetch,                    // optional override (e.g. for testing)
})

params.name: apiKey
params.description: Your Talonic API key. Starts with `tlnc_`.

params.name: maxRetries
params.description: Maximum retry attempts for transient errors. Default: `3`.

params.name: fetch
params.description: Custom fetch implementation for testing or proxying.

title: Extract
seoTitle: SDK Extract Method â€” Talonic Docs
description: The top-level talonic.extract() method for single-call document extraction with schema validation.

content.text: The top-level `extract()` method is the fastest way to get structured data from a document. Provide a file and a schema, get back validated JSON.

content.title: Basic extraction

content.text: You can also pass `file_url`, `schema_id` (for saved schemas), or a full JSON Schema definition.

title: Usage
seoTitle: Talonic CLI Usage â€” Talonic Docs
description: Use the talonic CLI binary for command-line extraction, schema management, and document listing.

content.text: The package ships with a `talonic` binary for command-line usage:

content.title: CLI examples
content.code:
talonic schemas list
talonic documents list --per-page=20
talonic extract ./invoice.pdf \
  --schema='{"vendor_name":"string","total_amount":"number"}'
talonic --help

faq.question: Does the Talonic SDK include a CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The @talonic/node package ships a talonic binary for command-line extraction, schema management, and document operations.

title: Error Classes
seoTitle: SDK Error Classes â€” Talonic Docs
description: Typed error hierarchy for the Talonic Node SDK: TalonicError base class with subclasses for auth, validation, rate limit, server, network, and timeout errors.

content.text: Every failure is a `TalonicError` subclass with `code`, `status`, `requestId`, and `message` properties:

params.name: TalonicRateLimitError
params.description: Rate limit exceeded (after retries exhausted). Includes `rateLimit.resetAt`.

content.title: Error handling

faq.question: What error types does the Talonic SDK throw?
faq.answer: TalonicAuthError (401/403), TalonicNotFoundError (404), TalonicValidationError (400/409/413/422), TalonicRateLimitError (429), TalonicServerError (5xx), TalonicNetworkError, and TalonicTimeoutError.

title: Retries
seoTitle: SDK Retry Behavior â€” Talonic Docs
description: Automatic retry behavior for the Talonic Node SDK: exponential backoff with jitter on transient errors and rate limits.

content.text: The SDK retries automatically on `429` (respecting `X-RateLimit-Reset`), `500`, `502`, `503`, `504`, network errors, and timeouts. Backoff is exponential with jitter, capped at 16 seconds.
The API may set `retryable: false` on a specific error; the SDK respects that and does not retry.
Configure the maximum number of retries via the `maxRetries` constructor option (default: `3`).

faq.question: Does the SDK retry failed requests?
faq.answer: Yes. Automatic retries on 429, 500, 502, 503, 504, network errors, and timeouts with exponential backoff capped at 16s. Configure via maxRetries (default: 3).
